channel.title: Life Lens System — Discovery Timeline
channel.description: New discoveries, insights and components as they emerge. One line per breakthrough.

item.title: Theta-Myelin: The Myelination Engine
item.description: Highways are artificially myelinated paths. The brain does not search — it fires along the thickest connections. ThetaOS now emulates this: every knowledge card retrieval is logged, building a dataset of co-occurring circuits. Three signal types determine which paths earn myelin: timing errors (cerebellum equivalent), cognitive errors (anterior cingulate cortex equivalent), and reinforcement signals (dopamine equivalent). Priority follows consequence of absence, not frequency of use. The myelination engine is now measuring itself.

item.title: Epistemic Dimension: three implicit mechanisms made explicit
item.description: The 12-layer model had three epistemic mechanisms it never named. Replacement chains: when a standpoint replaces an older one, the evolution itself is navigable knowledge — recursive queries walk the chain in both directions. Epistemic transformation labeling: a direct quote is not an interpretation is not a synthesis — each step away from the source changes the certainty. Belief time: when you started believing something and when you stopped, independent of when it happened in the world or when it was logged. Built and tested on live data. Triggered by reading an external knowledge architecture proposal and discovering what it was missing.

item.title: Predictive Coding: the system fires before you arrive
item.description: Meetings and optredens in the next seven days now activate networks in advance — before departure, while there is still time to prepare. Two Den Haag meetings triggered Politie Den Haag and Den Haag cluster the evening before. Retrospective signals tell where you were. Predictive signals tell where you are going. Brain mechanism 22.

item.title: Receptive Field: where the system knows, and where it is silent
item.description: Every location has a receptive field — the network of people, events and transactions that fires when you enter that place. Elp: 134 people, three networks, fires immediately. Limburg: silent. The sensory territory map reveals the shape of what is missing. A blank space is not nothing — it is an instruction. Brain mechanism 21.

item.title: Choroid Plexus: the enveloping immune system of the network
item.description: Joy Otten named the missing piece during a live demo for law enforcement at Troi, Noordwijk. Not a sixth layer stacked on top — the membrane wrapped around everything. Filtration, nightly cleansing, immune patrol, homeostasis. Later that day: the Obsidian vault (35,000 files, 12,680 hand-validated entities) turned out to already BE the choroid plexus.

item.title: Double Helix: evidence and meaning as two strands through every synapse
item.description: Every synapse carries two strands: how strong is the proof (evidence) and what does it mean (fact, belief, or hypothesis). Layer 10 — human confirmation — is the helicase that opens and reseals them. Emerged from cross-pollination with Mark Vletter's Klai system.

item.title: Action Potential: the network stores everything but can't fire
item.description: Demo with Joe and Mika Ros revealed the gap: three layers describe how synapses are stored, strengthened and enriched. None describes how they're retrieved. Six sub-mechanisms: threshold, saltatory conduction, payload, excitation/inhibition, summation, refractory period.

item.title: Diamond Layer: eight dimensions per synapse
item.description: Temporality, directionality, valence, weight, social graph, context, decay, hub value. Not just connected — multidimensional. Emerged at 07:10 after the third night of building.

item.title: Theta-Myelin: completeness as insulation
item.description: A thin synapse (name only) fires slowly. A thick synapse (name + date + location + context + photo) fires instantly. Myelin grows through more layers, more frequency, and more completeness per mention.

item.title: Synaptic Stratification: ten levels of evidence
item.description: Not every connection is equally certain. Ten layers from intentional contact (100% certain) to pattern recognition (50-70%). 170,000+ measured synapses. The trust model for connections.

item.title: Tom is born: a personal AI guide on top of ThetaOS
item.description: Not a chatbot. A coach, advisor, strategist, biographer and archivist with access to an entire life. First external user: Kees Verhoeven (March 14).

item.title: ThetaOS gets a name — and becomes a Life Lens System
item.description: The system that had been growing for months finally got its name. And almost immediately revealed itself as something more than a tool: a way of looking at an entire life through structured, connected data.

item.title: Gmail archive parsed: 96,000 emails reveal a hidden system
item.description: 14GB of email, converted with Python scripts written with AI. The data was always there. The connections were waiting to be made.

item.title: First book written with AI: Starten met Obsidian
item.description: After the stroke, during the first public demo of Obsidian, someone asked a question that sparked the idea. Six weeks later the book was in stores. First solo book, AI as writing partner.

item.title: Personal ontology written down — then a stroke
item.description: January 2025. For the first time, the complete structure of how I organise information was made explicit. Then the enforced slowness of recovery turned out to be a gift.
